commit.message:
fix: refresh upstream tokens transparently instead of forcing re-auth (#4036)

* Refresh upstream tokens transparently instead of forcing re-auth

The upstreamswap middleware returned 401 when upstream access tokens
expired, forcing users through full re-authentication even though
valid refresh tokens existed in storage. This happened because:

1. Redis/memory storage TTL was set to access token expiry, deleting
   the entry (and refresh token) when the access token expired
2. Storage returned nil on ErrExpired, discarding the refresh token
3. The middleware had no refresh path — only 401

Fix all three layers:

- Add DefaultRefreshTokenTTL (30 days) to storage entry TTL so
  refresh tokens survive past access token expiry
- Return token data alongside ErrExpired from storage so callers
  can use the refresh token
- Add UpstreamTokenRefresher interface and implementation that wraps
  the upstream OAuth2Provider and storage
- Plumb the refresher through Server → EmbeddedAuthServer → Runner →
  MiddlewareRunner
- Update upstreamswap middleware to attempt refresh before returning
  401, only requiring re-auth when the refresh token itself fails

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add unit tests for token refresh paths

Add comprehensive tests for RefreshAndStore (6 cases) and middleware
refresh paths (4 cases: successful refresh, failed refresh, no refresh
token, defense-in-depth expired-without-error).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Update pkg/authserver/refresher.go

Co-authored-by: Jakub Hrozek <jakub.hrozek@posteo.se>

* Update pkg/auth/upstreamswap/middleware.go

Co-authored-by: Jakub Hrozek <jakub.hrozek@posteo.se>

* Address PR review comments

- Fix step numbering: renumber step 6 → 5 after step 5 removal
- Update redis integration test: assert returned token data is non-nil
  on ErrExpired, consistent with the unit test contract
- Fix test closures: pass subtest t to setupStorage/setupProvider to
  ensure assertion failures are attributed to the correct subtest

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add singleflight for concurrent refresh deduplication and fix lint

Wrap upstream token refresh in singleflight.Group keyed on sessionID
to collapse concurrent refreshes into one call. Prevents providers with
single-use refresh tokens from failing all but the first concurrent
caller.

Added TestSingleFlightRefresh_ConcurrentRequests to verify the fix.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Update middleware.go

Co-authored-by: Jakub Hrozek <jakub.hrozek@posteo.se>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: Jakub Hrozek <jakub.hrozek@posteo.se>

commit.message:
Add pre_pre_process hook to expand \BIT/\EIT environment macros for latex2html

Fixes long-standing l2h issue (noted in stddefs.tex line 143) where
latex2html cannot parse \BIT, \EIT, \BNUM, \ENUM and their lowercase/compact
variants. The hook expands them to \begin{itemize}/\end{itemize} etc.
before l2h's environment parser runs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: add vercel.json with SPA routing and cache headers

- Rewrite all routes to index.html (fixes direct navigation 404s)
- Set Cache-Control: no-cache on index.html so browsers always fetch
  the latest version after a new deployment (fixes stale index.html
  referencing old chunk hashes → all /assets/*.js returning 404)
- Set Cache-Control: immutable on /assets/* for optimal CDN performance

commit.message:
Add Wonder Profile section — book-accurate design system replica

Implements the exact design language of the physical book as web
components, creating a seamless "preview a page" experience:

- Rich cosmic nebula background with CSS starfield overlay
- Two-column book spread layout (left/right page)
- Gold-bordered glass panels (glassmorphism + #FFD700 borders)
- Cinzel serif typography in gold for titles, all-caps
- Rank badge pill with gold outline
- Stats row: 3 colored circles (Beauty=pink, Power=orange,

commit.message:
Report view mismatch instead of generic timeout in oracle shape checker (#3984)

## Summary

Improve oracle test error reporting by showing the actual view mismatch
instead of a generic timeout message.

## Problem

When the oracle shape checker timed out waiting for consistency, it
called `flunk("Timeout waiting for shape=...")` which gave no
information about what the actual mismatch was. This made debugging
oracle test failures harder than necessary — you'd see a timeout error
when the real problem was a data discrepancy.

## Solution

On timeout, instead of calling `flunk()`, the checker now returns the
current state and lets `assert_consistent!` compare the views and report
the actual difference. This way, test failures show which rows or values
diverged rather than just "timed out".

## Test Plan

- [x] Code compiles cleanly with `--warnings-as-errors`
- [x] Formatting passes
- [x] Oracle tests exercise this path (tagged `:oracle`, require special
infrastructure)

---
Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(agent): use field-by-field resolution for outputLimitConfig

Align outputLimitConfig precedence strategy with prefixConfig and treeConfig
by resolving each field individually with ?? instead of using whole-object ??.
This ensures that a partial outputLimitConfig (e.g. only `enabled`) still
falls through to flat fields for missing properties like `limitPercent`.

   is only included when provided, avoiding PrismaClientValidationError
   if the generated client is stale.

2. Anchor service: catch TRANSACTION_REPLACED errors from tx.wait() and
   check receipt.status === 1 — if the replacement tx succeeded, treat
   as success. Use receipt.hash (confirmedTxHash) throughout the confirm
   path so DB records store the actual confirmed tx hash.

commit.message:
refactor(agent): extract LLMCallLifecycle from Agent (#472)

* refactor(agent): extract LLMCallLifecycle from Agent

* fix(agent): skip afterLLMCall controller for interrupted calls

The `completeLLMCall()` refactoring introduced a behavioral change: by
delegating the finally-block cleanup path to `completeLLMCall()`, the
`afterLLMCall` controller now fired for interrupted calls, which the
original Agent code never did.

Guard `processAfterLLMCallController` to skip controller invocation
when `finishReason === "interrupted"`, restoring the original behavior.
This prevents controller side effects (e.g., `append_messages` mutating
conversation state) during cleanup of an already-exited run loop.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: ignore .cascade/ session context directory

Add .cascade/ to .gitignore to prevent Cascade tooling session
artifacts (pre-fetched context files, etc.) from appearing as
untracked files.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add optimizer composite tool E2E test with fake embedding server (#4059)

The existing optimizer E2E test uses the heavyweight TEI container image
for its embedding server, which is slow to pull and start. This adds a
parallel test that uses a lightweight fake embedding server (Python HTTP
server mimicking the TEI /info and /embed endpoints) while exercising
composite tool discovery and invocation through the optimizer's find_tool
and call_tool meta-tools.

The new DeployFakeEmbeddingServer helper can be reused by other tests
that need optimizer mode without the TEI dependency.
